常見問題：

AC米蘭同國際米蘭又係有"米蘭"，應該點分？
ANS：AC米蘭簡稱米蘭，國際米蘭叫國米，避免稱呼做AC，因為AC係球會咁解，而milan先係名既簡稱。

AC米蘭仲係咪「老人院」？
ANS：現時全隊平均年齡為25歲。

今個轉會窗到目前為止簽咗咁多球員，遲啲仲會唔會有錢買人？
ANS：其實買球員好少會一炮過畀，多數都係分期，所以仲會有錢簽其他球員。

簽咗咁多球員會唔會過唔到FFP？
ANS：早前米蘭管理層有同UEFA探討經營計劃。
http://sempremilan.com/ts-fassone-meets-uefa-to-discuss-ac-milan-business-plan（By 二石四鳥）

Federico Manasse @FedericoManasse
‏
Riccardo Saponara's touching tribute to his captain Davide Astori, on Instagram. Translated from Italian to English in this thread. I had to do this because it is just too beautiful and heartwarming of a message to be ignored. Do read it.

"Oh captain, my captain. Why did you not come down to have breakfast with us all? Why did you not pick up your shoes from outside of Marco (Sportiello)’s room and then drink your orange juice, as usual? Now they’ll tell us that life goes on, that we must look forward and pick ourselves up, but what will your absence taste like? Who will arrive every morning in the cafeteria, warming up everyone with his smile? Who will ask us about what we did the previous night and have a laugh about it? Who will nurture the youngsters and give a sense of responsibility to the veterans? Who will form the circle to work on our ‘two-touch play’ and who will demolish Marco on the PlayStation? With whom will we debate about Masterchef, Florence’s restaurants, TV series or games played? Who will I lean on at lunch after a tiring training session? Come on, come back. You still need to finish watching LaLaLand to analyse like you did with all new movies. Come back to Florence, they’re waiting for you to renew your contract and acknowledge all the good and the positivity that you bring to us on a daily basis. Get out of that damn room, we’ll be waiting for you tomorrow at training. In life there are people you’ve known forever but have never bonded with, and then there are the 'Davides', who warm to you immediately with a simple ‘Welcome to Florence, Ricky’. Wherever you are now, keep on defending our goal and enlighten the right path for us from the backline. Oh captain, my captain. Forever, my captain. 💜"

睇到標眼水

Three dates for Milan derby

There are three possible dates for the rescheduled Derby della Madonnina, reports suggest, but it depends on how Juventus and Milan progress in Europe.

Sunday’s clash between Milan and Inter was postponed due to the tragic death of Davide Astori, as were all Serie A games, but the derby is proving harder to reschedule.

While the other games will be played on April 3 and 4, Milan will be in Europa League action that week if they progress to the quarter-finals.

According to today’s Corriere della Sera, the match could be played on Wednesday April 25, which is a public holiday for Liberation Day if the Diavolo don’t make it to the Europa League semi-finals.

There is also the possibility of playing on May 9, and moving the Coppa Italia final to May 25.

However, there is also Juventus to consider as they face the Diavolo in the Coppa and the Champions League final is May 26.

Should both teams reach the final in their respective European competitions - the Europa League final is May 16 - then the derby could be May 20, pushing Week 38 back to the following Wednesday.

AC Milan target Ki Sung-yueng drops hint over what his Swansea City future holds

The South Korea has decided to put a decision over where he will play next season on the backburner

Ki Sung-yueng says there is “a big possibility” he could sign a new contract at Swansea City after setting the club on the way to a vital victory over West Ham.

The South Korea scored his second goal in three Premier League games as Carlos Carvalhal’s side eased to a 4-1 win over the Hammers to climb out of the relegation zone.

The midfielder has been in impressive form, but his future is uncertain with his contract set to expire at the end of the season.

After talks with Carvalhal, Ki agreed to put a decision over where he will play next season on the backburner until the Swans know what division they will be playing in.

He was linked with West Ham in January, with Italian giants AC Milan also reportedly interested.

The 29-year-old remains noncommittal when asked about his next move — preliminary talks over an extension started but were put on hold — and he revealed he is giving strong consideration to extending his six-year stay in SA1.

“I had a conversation with Carlos in January because I wasn’t sure about my future with only six months left on my contract,” said Ki.

“We had a good chat and, because I am a professional, I promised him I would work hard to the end of the season.

“I don’t want to focus on any other teams. I want to make sure Swansea stay in the Premier League – that’s all I want to do.

“I can think about my future after the final game of the season. I can’t decide now because now it is time to work for the team.

“I have to help Swansea stay in the league – it is always the team before me. I am an honest guy. I don’t lie to anyone, the manager or my team mates.

“I gave him my promise that I would keep on working for him.

“I can’t predict my future. I am not sure if any teams will come in for me. After the season I have to think about that.

“I have been here for six years and I want to be respectful to Swansea. I have good memories with the fans, my team-mates and the club.

“I can’t say 100% what my decision will be, but I will be here until the summer and then I will have to talk to the club and see what happens.

“There is a big possibility I could sign a new contract here, that’s why I can’t say what I am going to do after the season ends.”
Conti is very close to returning. He could play 90 minutes with the Primavera this weekend. He could return to the first team after the international break against Juventus.

Report: Reina to Milan done


It’s reported Napoli goalkeeper Pepe Reina has officially accepted a move to Milan, who have informed his current club.

The Spanish international is out of contract at the end of the season, and the Partenopei were only willing to offer him a one-year extension.

In recent weeks it has been reported that the Rossoneri were keen to sign him, and today’s Gazzetta dello Sport reports that a deal is done.

Under FIFA regulations, any club can approach Reina in the last six months of his contract, but they must inform his current club when a deal is reached.

Gazzetta reports that the Diavolo have informed Napoli that a contract has been agreed until 2020 for the goalkeeper, who turns 36 in August.

The newspaper believes the contract is worth €3m per season, plus bonuses, and there is an option for an additional year.

An announcement is not expected imminently though, as Milan still have to resolve the future of Gianluigi Donnarumma.

There have been rumours the 19-year-old will be sold this summer, and an announcement of Reina's arrival would only add fuel to the fire.

Milan clubs vie for Kovacic

It’s reported both Inter and Milan are interested in signing Real Madrid’s Mateo Kovacic this summer.

The Croatian previously played for the Nerazzurri, but moved to Spain in a €29m deal in the summer of 2016.

According to Tuttosport, Inter tried to bring him back in January, but Los Merengues shut the door on any deal.

However, sporting director Piero Ausilio is still keen to get a deal done and will try again in the summer.

They could face competition from their city rivals though, with the newspaper stating that the Rossoneri are looking for a midfielder with his qualities.

Sporting director Massimiliano Mirabelli worked at Inter previously, and is known to be a big admirer of Kovacic.

The issue will be cost, however, as the Diavolo still don’t know what restrictions will be imposed by UEFA through Financial Fair Play.
